title: 10 best herbal creams for chronic hepatitis

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Milk thistle creams helps with chronic hepatitis because of its potent antioxidant properties, particularly silymarin, which has been shown to protect liver cells from damage caused by free radicals. The cream's anti-inflammatory effects also help to reduce liver inflammation and scarring associated with the disease. Additionally, milk thistle's ability to stimulate bile production can aid in the removal of toxins and waste products from the body, further supporting liver health and function. Overall, herbal milk thistle creams offer a natural and holistic approach to managing chronic hepatitis symptoms.
remedy_properties: Silymarin: Acts as an antioxidant to reduce liver damage and inflammation, thereby improving liver function and overall health.
Flavonoids: Help protect the liver from further damage by scavenging free radicals and inhibiting the activity of pro-inflammatory enzymes, reducing oxidative stress.
Silipide: Enhances the absorption and bioavailability of silymarin, increasing its effectiveness in treating chronic hepatitis by delivering it directly to the liver tissue.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in silymarin, a flavonoid complex that helps protect liver cells and reduce inflammation associated with chronic hepatitis.
Seeds: Contain silybin, a potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compound that has been shown to improve liver function and reduce damage caused by hepatitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry 100 grams of fresh silybum marianum leaves and stems for use in the cream.
Grind 50 grams of dried silybum marianum into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine the powdered silybum marianum with 20 grams of sweet almond oil, 10 grams of beeswax, and 5 grams of coconut oil in a heat-proof bowl.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the wax has melted and the mixture reaches 160°f.
Allow the cream to cool and thicken for at least 2 hours before transferring it to sterilized containers and storing it in the refrigerator.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with chronic hepatitis because they contain anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds that can help reduce liver damage and inflammation. The cream's active ingredients, such as taraxasterol and sesquiterpenoids, have been shown to possess immunomodulatory effects, which can aid in the modulation of the immune response and reduce oxidative stress. Additionally, dandelion creams may help to improve liver function by increasing bile flow and reducing liver scarring, making them a potential adjunctive therapy for managing chronic hepatitis symptoms.
remedy_properties: Choleretic and cholagogue effects: Dandelion creams stimulate the production of bile, which helps to reduce liver inflammation and improve digestion, ultimately alleviating symptoms of chronic hepatitis.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids and phenolic acids present in dandelion cream have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help protect the liver from further damage, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation associated with chronic hepatitis.

Detoxification support: Dandelion root has been traditionally used to support the body's natural detoxification processes, which can help remove toxins and excess bile acids that may accumulate in the liver due to chronic hepatitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, which have been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce liver damage and inflammation associated with chronic hepatitis.

Rhizomes: Rhizomes of dandelion contain a high concentration of inulin, a prebiotic fiber that helps promote the growth of beneficial gut bacteria, which is essential for immune system function and overall health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest taraxacum officinale roots and dried them in a warm place for 7 days.
Combine 20 grams of dried taraxacum officinale roots with 500ml of olive oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at low temperature for 4 hours while stirring occasionally to create a tincture.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container and add 100 grams of beeswax.
Melt the mixture by placing it in a water bath at 60°c for 30 minutes before whipping and filling cream tubes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice creams helps with chronic hepatitis because they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. When applied topically to the skin, glycyrrhizin can help reduce liver inflammation and improve liver function by increasing the production of glutathione, a crucial antioxidant that helps protect cells from damage. Additionally, licorice creams may also help modulate the immune response, reducing the severity of symptoms associated with chronic hepatitis. As a result, licorice creams have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to support liver health and alleviate the discomfort of chronic hepatitis.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: Has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help reduce liver inflammation and oxidative stress caused by chronic hepatitis.
Flavonoids: Have antiviral and immunomodulatory effects, which can help combat the viral infection and modulate the immune response to alleviate symptoms of chronic hepatitis.
Licoricidin: Has anti-inflammatory and anti-fibrotic effects, which can help reduce liver fibrosis (scarring) caused by chronic hepatitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain compounds like glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, making them effective in soothing liver inflammation and reducing viral replication in chronic hepatitis.

Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are the underground stems of the licorice plant that contain saponins, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to protect the liver from further damage and promoting its natural healing process.
remedy_recipe: Grind 200 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Combine the powdered glycyrrhiza glabra root with 100 milliliters of distilled water in a heat-resistant bowl and simmer for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass jar, discarding the solids and retaining the liquid extract.
Mix the resulting liquid extract with 20 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler, melting the ingredients for 15 minutes.
Pour the warm cream mixture into sterilized glass jars and allow it to cool and set at room temperature for 2 hours before storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea creams helps with chronic hepatitis because they provide a rich source of antioxidants, anti-inflammatory compounds, and immunomodulatory agents that help alleviate symptoms and slow disease progression. Herbal teas such as licorice root, dandelion, and turmeric have been traditionally used to support liver health by reducing inflammation, improving bile flow, and enhancing antioxidant defenses. By incorporating these tea creams into one's daily routine, individuals with chronic hepatitis may experience improved liver function, reduced symptoms, and enhanced overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Herbal teas like milk thistle, turmeric, and ginger have antioxidant properties that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the liver, which can alleviate symptoms of chronic hepatitis.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbs like chamomile, peppermint, and licorice root have anti-inflammatory compounds that may help reduce liver inflammation and improve liver function in people with chronic hepatitis.

Immune-modulating properties: Herbal teas like echinacea and astragalus contain immune-boosting compounds that may enhance the body's natural defenses against viral hepatitis, such as Hepatitis B and C.
remedy_parts: Roots: Containing high amounts of saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective in reducing liver inflammation and protecting against oxidative damage.
Barks: Rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to possess antiviral and antimicrobial properties, helping to combat hepatitis-causing viruses and bacteria.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried camellia sinensis leaves and grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 2 minutes.
Combine the ground camellia sinensis powder with 200ml of cold-pressed coconut oil in a heat-proof glass beaker.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at low temperature for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the powder dissolves and an emulsion forms.
Add 20g of beeswax and 10ml of sweet almond oil to the emulsion, heating gently while stirring until the wax melts and the mixture thickens.
Pour the cream into sterilized glass jars, seal tightly, and store in a cool place for up to 6 months or in the refrigerator for up to 1 year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Phyllanthus creams helps with chronic hepatitis because they contain potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that directly target the liver. The creams' active ingredients, such as flavonoids and terpenoids, work to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, thereby slowing down liver damage and fibrosis. Additionally, Phyllanthus extracts have been shown to stimulate the immune system, promoting a natural response to viral clearance and improving overall liver function. This unique combination of actions makes herbal Phyllanthus creams an effective adjunctive therapy for managing chronic hepatitis symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antiviral activity: Phyllanthus amarus cream has been shown to inhibit the replication of hepatitis B virus (HBV) and reduce viral load, helping to control the progression of chronic hepatitis.

Immune modulatory effects: The cream's immunomodulatory properties can help regulate an overactive immune response, which is often associated with chronic liver inflammation and damage caused by HBV infection.

Antioxidant activity: Phyllanthus amarus cream's antioxidant properties can help reduce oxidative stress and liver damage caused by free radicals, which are often elevated in patients with chronic hepatitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help combat liver damage and inflammation associated with chronic hepatitis.

Rhyzomes: Contain bioactive compounds like phyllanthin and amarogentin, which have been shown to possess antiviral and anti-inflammatory activities, making them effective in managing chronic hepatitis symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of phyllanthus amarus and wash them thoroughly in cold water.
Dry the leaves using a food dehydrator at 60°c for 12 hours or air-drying method for 3 days.
Combine 30g dried phyllanthus amarus leaves with 100ml coconut oil in a double boiler and heat for 4 hours.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth to remove solids and filter it again using a coffee filter paper.
Pour the filtered cream into sterilized jars, seal them tightly, and store in the refrigerator for up to 3 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tree turmeric creams helps with chronic hepatitis because they contain curcumin, a potent anti-inflammatory compound that targets liver inflammation and oxidative stress. Curcumin's antioxidant properties also help neutralize free radicals, reducing liver damage and promoting its natural regeneration process. Additionally, turmeric's bioactive compounds may modulate the immune system, suppressing pro-inflammatory cytokines and promoting Th1/Th2 balance, thereby alleviating chronic hepatitis symptoms such as fatigue, jaundice, and abdominal pain.
remedy_properties: Curcumin: Curcumin, a polyphenol compound found in turmeric, has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce liver inflammation and oxidative stress associated with chronic hepatitis.

Cyclooxygenase inhibition: Turmeric cream's cyclooxygenase (COX) inhibitory activity helps reduce the production of pro-inflammatory eicosanoids, which can contribute to liver damage and fibrosis in chronic hepatitis patients.

Immunomodulatory effects: The immunomodulatory compounds present in turmeric creams help modulate the immune response, reducing excessive inflammation and promoting a balanced immune function that is beneficial for managing chronic hepatitis.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzome, which is a type of underground stem, contains high levels of curcumin, a powerful anti-inflammatory compound that has been shown to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with chronic hepatitis.

Roots: Turmeric roots have been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for their antiviral and antimicrobial properties, making them effective against the viral strains that cause chronic hepatitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of dried berberis aristata root and mix it with 250ml of carrier oil in a clean glass jar.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 60°c for 3 hours, allowing the herbs to infuse into the oil.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into another clean glass container, discarding the solids and retaining the clear liquid.
Mix 100g of beeswax and 100g of coconut oil in a small saucepan and melt them over low heat, stirring occasionally.
Combine the infused oil with the melted wax-oil mixture and stir well, allowing it to cool and thicken into a creamy consistency within 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chebulic myrobalan creams helps with chronic hepatitis because of its potent anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory properties. The cream's active compounds, including chebulinic acid and gallic acid, have been shown to inhibit the activation of pro-inflammatory enzymes, reduce oxidative stress, and modulate immune responses. This multifaceted action helps to alleviate symptoms such as fatigue, joint pain, and skin rashes commonly associated with chronic hepatitis, while also supporting liver health and reducing inflammation in the affected organ.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant property: Chebulic Myrobalan cream's antioxidant property helps neutralize free radicals that damage liver cells and contribute to chronic hepatitis progression, thereby reducing inflammation and oxidative stress.

Anti-inflammatory property: The anti-inflammatory property of Chebulic Myrobalan cream reduces inflammation in the liver, which is a hallmark symptom of chronic hepatitis, and also alleviates associated symptoms such as fatigue, fever, and joint pain.

Antiviral property: The antiviral activity of Chebulic Myrobalan cream helps inhibit the replication of viral particles that cause hepatitis, reducing the severity of the condition and preventing further liver damage.
remedy_parts: Fruits: The fruits of the Chebulic Myrobalan plant contain potent antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which help soothe liver inflammation and promote healing in chronic hepatitis.
Roots: The roots of the plant have been traditionally used to treat liver disorders, including chronic hepatitis, due to their ability to stimulate liver function and reduce oxidative stress.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality terminalia chebula fruits and dry them completely under shade for at least seven days.
Grind the dried terminalia chebula fruits into fine powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder and sift it through a fine-mesh sieve.
Mix 200 grams of the powdered terminalia chebula with 300 grams of coconut oil and 100 grams of beeswax in a double boiler over low heat for two hours.
Add 20 milliliters of olive oil and 10 milliliters of lavender essential oil to the mixture and stir well to combine before removing from heat.
Allow the cream base to cool and thicken completely at room temperature before transferring it to an airtight container for storage.

Roots: Rich in volatile oils and other bioactive compounds that have been traditionally used to treat liver and gallbladder disorders, making them useful for managing symptoms of chronic hepatitis.
remedy_recipe: Gather and dry turmeric roots curcuma longa for subsequent infusion and blending.
Boil one liter of water and infuse one tablespoon of dried turmeric root for twenty minutes.
Strain the infused liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container.
Combine the filtered liquid with one hundred grams of unrefined coconut oil and mix until emulsified.
Store the cream in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger creams helps with chronic hepatitis because of their unique combination of anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive compounds present in ginger, such as gingerols and shogaols, have been shown to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the liver, which are common hallmarks of chronic hepatitis. remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality zingiber officinale roots, rinse them thoroughly and chop into small pieces.
Combine 30g of chopped ginger with 100ml of olive oil in a double boiler, heat for 3 hours.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle, squeezing out as much oil as possible.
Add 20ml of coconut oil and 10g of beeswax to the ginger-infused oil, stir until fully incorporated.
Pour the herbal cream into sterilized jars, seal tightly and store in the refrigerator for up to 6 weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chicory creams helps with chronic hepatitis because they contain saponins, a type of phytochemical that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties. These anti-inflammatory compounds help to reduce liver inflammation and damage caused by chronic hepatitis, thereby improving liver function and reducing symptoms such as fatigue, nausea, and abdominal pain. Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in chicory cream, including polyphenols and saponins, can neutralize free radicals that contribute to liver cell damage and fibrosis associated with chronic hepatitis.

Immune-modulating properties: Chicory's bioactive compounds may help regulate the immune response, reducing excessive inflammation and promoting a balanced immune system function, which is crucial for managing chronic hepatitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the chicory plant are rich in inulin, a prebiotic fiber that helps to regulate liver function and reduce inflammation associated with chronic hepatitis.
Barks: The barks of the chicory plant contain saponins, which have been shown to exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate symptoms of chronic hepatitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality cichorium intybus leaves and dry them thoroughly in shaded areas for 7-10 days.
Grind the dried cichorium intybus leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix 2 teaspoons of the ground cichorium intybus with 1 tablespoon of coconut oil in a small saucepan over low heat for 10-15 minutes.
Stir in 1 teaspoon of beeswax and 1/2 teaspoon of vitamin e oil, then continue to heat mixture until wax is fully melted for 5 minutes.
Pour the warm cream base into glass jars, let it cool and thicken at room temperature for 30-40 minutes before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow creams helps with chronic hepatitis because they soothe and calm the liver, reducing inflammation and discomfort. remedy_recipe: Harvest althaea officinalis leaves and flowers, drying them thoroughly within 24 hours to preserve potency.
Combine 250 grams of dried althaea officinalis plant material with 1000 milliliters of cold-pressed olive oil in a glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 14 days, shaking it twice daily to facilitate infusion.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids after 30 minutes.
Mix 20 milliliters of the althaea officinalis infused oil with 10 grams of beeswax and heat it gently in a double boiler until melted and fully incorporated.

Barks: Containing triterpenoid saponins, which have been shown to exhibit antiviral properties, helping to combat viral hepatitis infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest urtica dioica leaves and flowers at dawn when they are most potent for maximum potency.
Dry the harvested plant material using shade drying or oven drying at 30°c for 24 hours to preserve properties.
Infuse dried plant material in sweet almond oil at a ratio of 10:1 for 2 weeks with occasional stirring to extract active compounds.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth and mix it with beeswax and vitamin e oil in a 20% concentration for thickening and stability.
Allow the mixture to cool and solidify into a cream consistency, which can be stored in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe creams helps with chronic hepatitis because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The gel extracted from aloe vera plants has been shown to reduce inflammation in the liver, which is often a hallmark of chronic hepatitis. remedy_recipe: Gather aloe vera gel extract 200mg and combine it with coconut oil 20g.
Mix the aloe vera gel extract with beeswax 10g and shea butter 20g thoroughly.
Add licorice root powder 5g and dandelion root powder 5g to the mixture stir well.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at low temperature for 30 minutes until smooth consistency.
Fill sterile containers with the cream and refrigerate it for at least 24 hours before use.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rhus aromatica leaves and dry them for at least 24 hours to preserve their medicinal properties.
Grind dried rhus aromatica leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle, requiring approximately 10 minutes of grinding time.
Combine the powdered rhus aromatica with 20% beeswax and 30% coconut oil by weight, heating the mixture in a double boiler at 70°c for 1 hour to melt the wax.

Remove the mixture from heat and add 1 teaspoon of vitamin e oil and 0.5 teaspoons of tea tree essential oil, stirring until well combined and allowing it to cool and thicken at room temperature for 2 hours.
Pour the cooled cream mixture into sterilized glass jars and store them in the refrigerator for up to 6 months or use immediately after preparation.

remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry 50 grams of commiphora mukul resin in a well-ventilated area for 2 days.
Grind the dried resin into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix the powdered resin with 100 milliliters of organic coconut oil and 20 milliliters of beeswax at 65 degrees celsius for 30 minutes.
Stir the mixture occasionally until it thickens and reaches a consistency suitable for cream, taking about 1 hour and 15 minutes.
Pour the herbal cream into sterilized containers and store them in the refrigerator to slow down oxidation processes.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 tablespoons of dried chamomile flowers from your garden or store.
Combine the dried chamomile flowers with 1 tablespoon of potato starch in a mortar and pestle.
Grind the mixture into a fine powder for approximately 10 minutes.
Add 2 tablespoons of coconut oil and 1 tablespoon of distilled water to the powdered mixture.
Mix the ingredients thoroughly and store the cream in an airtight container for up to 7 days.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 500 grams of dried ashwagandha roots and grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix the powdered ashwagandha with 200 grams of petroleum jelly and 100 grams of coconut oil in a glass bowl until well combined.
Add 20 grams of beeswax pellets and 10 grams of vitamin e oil to the mixture and stir until fully incorporated for 5 minutes.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler setup at 150°f for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax is fully melted.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool and solidify at room temperature for 60 minutes before transferring to an airtight container for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: South American yerba creams helps with chronic hepatitis because of their unique blend of antioxidants, anti-inflammatory compounds, and antimicrobial properties. The creams' active ingredients, such as saponins and flavonoids, have been shown to reduce liver inflammation and fibrosis, thereby alleviating symptoms and slowing disease progression. Additionally, the creams' ability to improve bile flow and stimulate liver regeneration helps to restore normal liver function and promote overall health in individuals with chronic hepatitis.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activity: Yerba cream's antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the liver, while its anti-inflammatory effects alleviate symptoms such as pain, fatigue, and discomfort associated with chronic hepatitis.

Hepatoprotective and antiviral properties: Yerba cream has been shown to have a protective effect on liver cells, reducing damage caused by viruses and toxins, and its antiviral properties may help reduce the replication of hepatitis-causing viruses.

Immunomodulatory effects: Yerba cream's immunomodulatory properties help regulate the immune system's response to hepatitis infection, promoting a balanced and effective immune response that reduces inflammation and tissue damage.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with chronic hepatitis.
Barks: Contain phenolic compounds that have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective against liver damage.
Seeds: Abundant in essential fatty acids and vitamins, which help reduce inflammation and promote liver regeneration.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh achyrocline satureioides leaves and dry them in shade at room temperature for two weeks to preserve potency.
Grind dried leaves into fine powder using mortar and pestle, then sift through 100-mesh sieve to remove large particles.
Combine powdered achyrocline satureioides with 20% beeswax and 80% coconut oil in double boiler, stirring until mixture reaches temperature of 160°f.
Continue heating mixture for additional hour to enhance absorption and penetration properties, then remove from heat and let cool slightly.
Whip cooled mixture with hand blender or whisk until smooth and creamy, then pour into sterilized containers and store in refrigerator.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Blueberry creams helps with chronic hepatitis because of its unique combination of antioxidants, flavonoids, and anthocyanins. These powerful compounds work together to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the liver, which is a common characteristic of chronic hepatitis. The anti-inflammatory properties of blueberries also help to soothe and calm the liver, allowing it to function more efficiently and reducing scarring and damage caused by the disease. Additionally, the antioxidants in blueberry creams may help to combat liver damage caused by free radicals, promoting overall liver health and function.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Herbal blueberry creams rich in antioxidants such as anthocyanins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the liver, mitigating damage caused by chronic hepatitis.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in herbal blueberry creams, including quercetin and kaempferol, can help reduce liver inflammation and fibrosis associated with chronic hepatitis.

Antiviral properties: The antiviral compounds in herbal blueberry creams, such as gallic acid and ellagic acid, have been shown to inhibit the replication of viruses that cause hepatitis, including HBV and HCV.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, blueberry roots help reduce liver inflammation and oxidative stress associated with chronic hepatitis.
Barks: Containing flavonoids and phenolic acids, blueberry barks have been shown to exhibit antioxidant and antiviral properties, making them effective against viral hepatitis.
Seeds: High in fatty acids and vitamins, blueberry seeds may help improve liver function and reduce inflammation, alleviating symptoms of chronic hepatitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh vaccinium myrtillus berries and rinse them thoroughly under cold running water.
Blend the rinsed berries with 200ml of distilled water in a blender or food processor for 30 seconds.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a large bowl to extract the juice and pulp from the berries.
Mix 10g of beeswax and 20g of coconut oil with the vaccinium myrtillus juice in a double boiler over low heat for 30 minutes.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool slightly before pouring it into sterilized glass jars and refrigerating at 4°c.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for chronic hepatitis are topical preparations that harness the therapeutic properties of plant extracts to alleviate symptoms and improve liver health. These creams work by reducing inflammation, antioxidant activity, and improving immune function, all of which are essential for managing chronic hepatitis. Examples include creams containing turmeric, ginger, and green tea extract, which have been shown to reduce liver damage and fibrosis. For individuals with chronic hepatitis, using these herbal creams can improve their quality of life by reducing fatigue, abdominal pain, and itchy skin, allowing them to manage their condition more effectively and confidently.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat chronic hepatitis with herbal creams is by using a combination of potent herbs such as Milk Thistle, Dandelion, and Turmeric. These herbs have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce liver inflammation and promote healing. Apply the cream topically to affected areas, massaging gently to enhance absorption. Follow a consistent treatment regimen under the guidance of a healthcare professional for optimal results.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to chronic hepatitis that are treated with herbal creams are eczema, psoriasis, and acne. These conditions share similarities with chronic hepatitis in terms of inflammation, skin lesions, and oxidative stress. Herbal creams containing ingredients such as aloe vera, tea tree oil, and turmeric have been shown to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and soothe irritated skin, providing relief from these conditions.
